notes: DCs use Burnwillow scale: Routine(8), Hard(12), Heroic(16), Legendary(20). Damage types: fire, necrotic, poison, bludgeoning, slashing, piercing. Some hazards advance DoomClock or apply conditions instead of/in addition to damage.

1.name: Rot Pool
1.description: A shallow depression filled with grey-green liquid that smells of spoiled copper. Stepping in dissolves boot leather and bites into flesh. The pool pulses faintly, as if breathing.

1.name: Collapsing Beam
1.description: The ceiling groans. A timber the length of a man swings free from corroded brackets, slamming down on whatever crosses beneath. The dust cloud afterwards tastes of old fire.

1.name: Ember Geyser
1.description: A hairline crack in the floor vents superheated air from an Aether vein below. The stone around it is scorched black and faintly warm to the touch -- a half-second before it erupts.

1.name: Blight Spores
1.description: Clusters of pale mushrooms release a cloud of grey spores when disturbed by vibration. The spores sting exposed skin and burn the lungs. Covering your mouth helps. Mostly.

1.name: Rusted Floor
1.description: What appears to be solid stone is actually a thin crust over corroded metal grating. It gives way underfoot, and the jagged edges bite through boot soles.

1.name: Trapped Chest
1.description: A spring-loaded mechanism inside a salvaged chest, rigged by some long-dead scavenger who didn't want visitors. The needle is tipped with something brown and flaking.

1.name: Falling Rubble
1.description: The Burnwillow thrashes in its sleep. The ceiling cracks, and fist-sized chunks of petrified bark rain down. The shaking lasts three heartbeats, then stillness.

1.name: Slippery Ash
1.description: A thick layer of fine grey ash covers the floor like snow. It compresses to a frictionless surface underfoot. You don't realize you're falling until you're down.

2.name: Deep Rot Pool
2.condition: DC 12 Grit or early-stage Blight infection
2.description: A waist-deep depression of concentrated Blight fluid, its surface iridescent with decay. The liquid is warm and clings to skin. Immersion for more than a round risks infection.

2.name: Flame Jet
2.description: A ruptured Aether vein sprays pressurized sap-fire in a horizontal arc. The jet sweeps back and forth on a six-second interval. Timing the dash is the only way through.

2.name: Fungal Burst
2.condition: blinded for 1 round on failed save
2.description: A swollen puffball mushroom the size of a human head, stretched taut and glistening. A footstep's vibration is enough to rupture it. The cloud is thick, acrid, and utterly opaque.

2.name: Collapse Zone
2.description: A section of tunnel where the petrified bark has been weakened by root-rot. The floor sags visibly, and dust trickles from above. Running through triggers a full collapse.

2.name: Cursed Altar
2.description: A stone slab carved with pre-Arborist sigils, sticky with old offerings. Touching it sends a jolt of cold through the body, and somewhere deep in the tree, something stirs in response.

2.name: Mine Gas Pocket
2.condition: Extinguishes all open flames in the room
2.description: Invisible, odorless gas pooled in a low depression. A single spark from a torch or ember shard ignites the pocket in a flash-detonation that leaves ears ringing and eyebrows gone.

2.name: Ironvein Spike Trap
2.description: Iron spikes driven into a pivoting stone plate -- an Arborist mining-safety barrier repurposed as a weapon by something with hands and malice. The pressure plate is hidden under a layer of gravel.

3.name: Doom Fissure
3.description: A crack in the tunnel wall that exhales concentrated Blight energy as visible grey vapor. The air tastes of endings. Standing near it feels like remembering something terrible you've forgotten.

3.name: Magma Vent
3.description: A fissure in the stone floor where raw Aether has superheated the bedrock to molten slag. The air above it shimmers, and metal carried too close begins to glow cherry-red.

3.name: Blight Storm
3.condition: Exhaustion: -1 to all rolls until long rest
3.description: A localized weather event where Blight spores, rot-moisture, and fungal filaments swirl in a vortex that fills a corridor. Visibility drops to arm's length. Every breath is a gamble.

3.name: Rot Geyser
3.condition: DC 14 Grit or Blight infection (immediate stage 1)
3.description: A pressurized column of liquid Blight that erupts from the floor in timed intervals. The spray coats everything within ten feet in grey-green slime that seeps into open wounds.

3.name: Collapsing Floor to Void
3.condition: Victim falls to a lower zone. Party must find alternate route to regroup
3.description: The floor is a thin shell of petrified bark over a vertical shaft that drops into the zone below. The fall is survivable. What lives at the bottom may not be.

4.name: The Hollow King's Curse
4.condition: On failure, character cannot benefit from healing for 3 rounds
4.description: An invisible field of entropic will that radiates from sites sacred to the Hollow King. The body forgets how to mend. Wounds weep grey instead of red.

4.name: Ember Apocalypse Zone
4.condition: All gear takes 1 point of degradation per round of exposure
4.description: A chamber where a ruptured Aether main has ignited and cannot be quenched. The walls glow white-hot. Metal warps and leather chars. Even Tier III armor begins to soften at the edges.

4.name: DoomClock Surge
4.condition: DoomClock advances by 3. All party members must save individually
4.description: The DoomClock is not just a mechanic -- it is the tree's failing heartbeat. At critical mass, the Burnwillow convulses, and a wave of raw Blight energy pulses through every corridor. The Root-Song screams.

4.name: Rot Singularity
4.condition: On failure, character gains permanent Blight Stage 1 (requires Sun-Fruit to cure). DoomClock advances by 2
4.description: The heart of the infection. A point of absolute Rot concentration where reality itself seems to grey and thin. Colors fade. Sound dampens. The Root-Song is not silent here -- it is absent, as though it never existed.
